# Digital Employee Platform PRD

## Summary
Defines Ruh.ai as a horizontal digital employee operating system built on OpenClaw, including the core product thesis, trust-boundary model, and go-to-market shape.

## Extracted Preview
> Ruh.ai Platform Product Requirements Document
> Digital employee operating system built on OpenClaw gateway runtimes
> Document statusInternal draft for product, engineering, and solutions 
> leadership
> Version / date v1.0 | 7 March 2026
> Product decision in one sentence
> Ruh.ai should not be a collection of bespoke agents. It should be the horizontal platform that creates, deploys, 
> governs, and measures digital employees, while using OpenClaw as the runtime plane for sessions, routing, tools, 
> channels, sub-agents, hooks, and cron [1][2][5][8][9].
> Executive summary
> Dimension Decision
> Platform categoryDigital employee operating system built on OpenClaw runtime 
> gateways.
> Primary outcomeCreate, deploy, supervise, and improve collaborating digital 
> employees without bespoke engineering for every customer.
> Hard constraintOne OpenClaw gateway per tenant trust boundary; Ruh.ai must 
> not treat one shared gateway as a multi-tenant bus.
> Go-to-market patternHorizontal core platform plus vertical workflow packs, with 
> construction as the first industry pack.
> Why this architecture
> OpenClaw already gives Ruh.ai the gateway runtime primitives that matter: the gateway is the source of truth for 
> sessions, routing, and channel connections; each agent has isolated workspace/auth/session state; tools are typed 
> and policy-controlled; sub-agents and ACP sessions exist for delegation; hooks and cron exist for automation [1]
> [2][5][8][9]. The missing product is the enterprise control plane on top of those primitives.
